entries.description: Blocked clarified answers are not capped separately from safe rendered lines.

Implementation reuses existing clarification pending decisions and clarified-intent rendering while centralizing question creation behind EffectiveColonyMode()==orchestrator; default and legacy colony modes produce no pending decisions in focused coverage.
entries.suggestion: Preserve the central mode gate and source convention orchestrator:<workflow>:phase:<N>:<category>[:hard] when adding future boundary question categories.

entries.description: Runtime-owned Orchestrator boundary guidance is gated by EffectiveColonyMode()==orchestrator, derives pending questions from current unresolved clarification decisions, preserves after_discuss_next for normal lifecycle flow, and finalizers validate manifest root and colony_mode before later writes.
entries.suggestion: Preserve runtime ownership of orchestrator_boundary_guidance and keep wrappers consuming next/after_discuss_next instead of parsing pending-decisions.json or boundary_question_count.

entries.description: Normal Codex init orchestration still runs `aether init --charter-json ...` without asking the user to choose Colony Mode versus Orchestrator Mode or passing `--colony-mode`; the mirrored init YAML and creation skill show the same gap. As a result, the guided workflow cannot reach Orchestrator Mode unless the user already knows and supplies the raw flag, so the milestone goal to prompt for opt-in mode selection is only partially delivered.
entries.suggestion: Add an explicit mode-selection step to init command-guide, `.aether/commands/init.yaml`, Claude/OpenCode init wrappers, and the Codex creation skill; pass `--colony-mode <selected>` to runtime init, defaulting to `colony` when skipped or non-interactive.

entries.description: Codex init command-guide now tells the wrapper to ask for Colony Mode or Orchestrator Mode before state creation and defaults to Colony Mode when skipped or non-interactive.
entries.suggestion: Keep this mode-choice step adjacent to the runtime init command whenever init orchestration changes.

entries.description: The normal init run command now passes the selected mode into runtime with --colony-mode, so users can reach Orchestrator Mode through the guided front door.
entries.suggestion: Prefer a concrete selected_colony_mode variable in wrapper prose so generated commands do not drift from the runtime flag contract.

entries.description: TestInitGuideAndWrappersCarryColonyModeChoice covers command-guide, .aether init YAML, Claude/OpenCode init wrappers, and the Codex colony creation skill for Colony Mode, Orchestrator Mode, and --colony-mode anchors.
entries.suggestion: Keep this source-surface test in place for future init wrapper changes.

entries.description: Before publishing downstream, rebuild or publish the runtime so the installed aether binary reflects the updated source command-guide.
Proceed with seal from a quality/completeness perspective for the previously reported init opt-in blocker.
Installed runtime freshness remains a release-process concern until the updated source is built or published.
entries.suggestion: Add focused coverage before expanding the sealed surface.

entries.description: The command-guide run command uses a placeholder with a space, <selected colony|orchestrator>; it is understandable as documentation, but wrapper prose should continue storing a concrete selected_colony_mode value before execution.
entries.suggestion: Add focused coverage before expanding the sealed surface.

entries.description: The fixed path covers skipped or non-interactive mode choice by defaulting to Colony Mode while still allowing explicit Orchestrator Mode opt-in.
Wrapper parity matters at init, not only at downstream plan/build/continue/seal boundaries.
